metadata.database: ClimbMatch WA Ice Climbing Routes Hazard Database
quality_gates: Citation of data: elevation, aspect, slope angles, incident counts
Confidence levels: high (3+ sources), medium (2 sources), low (1 source)

routes.name: Example Route Name
routes.area: Geographic cluster (e.g., Banks Lake, Snoqualmie Pass)

hazards: Approach avalanche terrain: 30-40 degree slopes, north-facing aspect, wind slab development

watch_out: Avalanche terrain: 40-50 degree north-facing slope; soft slab formation in wind-loaded sections; seasonal trigger pattern: March-April during freeze-thaw cycles
Serac/icefall hazard: Upper pitch exposure to overhead ice flows; afternoon sun warming March+ creates falling ice risk; morning ascent essential during warm months
Ice quality: Formation season Dec-Feb; February best conditions with well-bonded ice; March onward becomes thinner and water-affected; years with low precipitation may see formation failure
Crevasse fields (if applicable): Random crevasses in approach glacier; late season (June+) with significant bridge collapse risk; probe continuously and rope up
Weather/wind: Summit winds 50-70 mph typical; afternoon storms build 2-4pm; whiteout visibility risk; wind slab development in surrounding terrain above 3,500 ft

Descent hazards: 2-pitch rappel to fixed anchors on rock ledge; inspect anchor quality before committing full weight (older slings may have reduced holding capacity); alternative down-climb option on climber's left if anchors compromised
Seasonal window: Best Jan-Feb when stable conditions; shoulder season (Dec early, March late) carries higher risk; April+ not recommended due to sun and instability

ice_conditions.water_dependency: Waterfall-dependent; low precipitation years may fail
ice_conditions.year_to_year_variability: High variability; lean years thin and difficult; fat years offer lower-angle sections

april_plus.risk_level: Very High or Not in condition

weather_patterns.wind_exposure: Fully exposed to westerly winds; summit-level wind funneling
weather_patterns.afternoon_storms: Thunderstorm development 2-4pm common June-September; less common Dec-Feb
weather_patterns.visibility: Whiteout risk in cloud cover; north-facing aspect often clear when valley fogged
weather_patterns.freeze_thaw: Good overnight freeze typical Jan-Feb; mid-day re-thaw March+

descent.anchor_locations: Fixed anchors on rock ledge above main flow; backup bolts on exposed rock to right
descent.anchor_quality_notes: Inspect slings before committing; weather-worn slings should be backed up with new webbing
descent.alternative_options: Down-climb option on climber's left if anchors compromised; walk-off upper ridge possible in good conditions
descent.route_finding_complexity: Straightforward in daytime; whiteout descent requires GPS/compass

access.road_conditions: Paved to X mile marker; may require 4WD after snowfall
access.access_notes: Raptor closure: Bridge Creek Wall area (1/2-mile buffer) closed January 1 - August 15 for golden eagle nesting

routes.notes: Route is well-documented with multiple recent sources. Hazards verified by 3+ independent reports.
